title: Unicode, Language, Tokenization, Fields, and Citation Offsets · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved trace contains a plausible reversible multilingual text pipeline with one ingestion, normalization, chunking, lexical, embedding, index, filter, fusion, reranking, context, generation, citation, authorization, latency, or recovery defect; identify the first failed stage before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reversible multilingual text pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.
Separate title, heading, body, code, identifier, date, author, tenant, and access metadata with explicit weighting and filter semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.text: A debug RAG engineering team handles case 2216 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to normalize and tokenize multilingual fields without losing source text or citation offsets; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-offset-lineage-1ctw4f
# Competency: rag-offset-lineage.
# Case 1490: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_offset_lineage_1ctw4f(original: str = "Café API v2", normalized: str = "café api v2", language: str = "fr", field_count: int = 3, offsets_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ctw4f = "1ctw4f-f4wtc1-1490"
    assert evidence_variant_1ctw4f
    evidence_axes_1ctw4f = (
        "corpus1ctw4f",
        "queryf4wtc1",
        "tenant1490",
        "index1ctw4f1490",
        "promptf4wtc11490",
        "evaluation14901ctw4f",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ctw4f)) == 6
    if not original or not normalized:
        return False, 0, "empty-text"
    if not language or field_count < 1:
        return False, field_count, "language-fields"
    if not offsets_preserved:
        return False, len(normalized), "citation-offsets"
    tokens = tuple(part for part in normalized.split() if part)
    if not tokens:
        return False, 0, "tokenization"
    assert "v2" in tokens
    return True, len(tokens), "unicode-field-model"
